title: Running-commentary comments inside useKonamiCode effect
summary: Seven line comments restate the next statement verbatim ('// Clear any existing timeout', '// Add key to sequence', etc.). Keep the leading KONAMI_CODE banner at line 5 since it names the sequence; drop the rest.
proposed_action: Delete the seven restatement comments at lines 55, 60, 63, 68, 74, 79, 86. Leave the line-5 KONAMI_CODE comment intact.

title: WebGL call-by-call restatement comments in WebGLSun
summary: Ten inline comments describe the next WebGL call rather than explaining intent ('// Set viewport', '// Clear canvas', '// Use program', '// Set uniforms', '// Draw', '// Bind position buffer', '// Bind texture coordinate buffer', '// Get locations', '// Animation loop', '// Start animation', '// Cleanup'). File has top-level section banners (Constants/Shaders/Component) already.
proposed_action: Delete the ten restatement comments. Keep the file-level banner comments (lines 6-8, 28-30, 46-48, 240-242) and JSDoc on the fragment shader (line 60).

title: Restatement comments in windowsSlice reducer
summary: Eight narrative comments restate the immediately following code. Keep 'SSR safety check' (57), 'Warn if more than 5 windows (soft limit)' (182), and the 'Re-opening' / 'If window exists but is closed, restore it' context hints — these add constraints not visible in the code.
proposed_action: Delete the eight restatement comments at lines 69, 73, 77, 83, 114, 121, 171, 178. Keep SSR-check, soft-limit warning, and re-opening hint comments.

title: useWindowManager wraps stable Zustand actions in zero-logic useCallbacks
summary: Nine useCallback wrappers (openWindow, openWindowWithZoom, closeWindow, focusWindow, toggleFullscreen, toggleWidget, updateWindowPosition, updateWindowSize, setActiveTab) do nothing but call the identically-named store action. Zustand actions are already referentially stable, so the wrappers add cost without benefit. toggleWindow (line 123) is the only useCallback with real logic. Comments at 62/74/80/156 restate the surrounding code.
proposed_action: Replace the nine trivial useCallbacks with direct store-action references (select them once and return). Keep toggleWindow's useCallback. Remove the four restatement comments at 62, 74, 80, 156.

title: apps/rad-os/components/ui/index.ts is a doc-only empty file with no exports
summary: The file contains a JSDoc block pointing readers to @rdna/radiants/components/core and exports nothing. A repo-wide search for `from '@/components/ui'` returns zero code importers (only archived migration docs). Consumers already deep-import @/components/ui/DesignSystemTab. The file's only value is a pointer comment; it is not reachable through imports.
proposed_action: Delete apps/rad-os/components/ui/index.ts. If the guidance still matters, move the note into a README or repo-level migration doc.

title: apps/rad-os/lib/mockData/index.ts is an unused single-file barrel
summary: apps/rad-os/lib/mockData/index.ts is `export * from './tracks'` alongside two restatement comments. The directory contains only `tracks.ts`. Both current importers deep-import `@/lib/mockData/tracks` — no caller goes through the barrel. It is a single-file barrel with zero importers.
proposed_action: Delete apps/rad-os/lib/mockData/index.ts. No consumers need updating.

title: apps/rad-os/lib/apps/index.ts is a documented single-file barrel
summary: Single-file barrel `export * from './catalog';`. Technically matches the brief's 'barrel files that only pass through a single module' rule, but it is widely imported (13+ `@/lib/apps` call sites) and was established as an intentional app-catalog public boundary (see archive/plans/2026-03-19-rados-app-catalog-boundary.md). Reporting so the coordinator can decide between 'collapse to @/lib/apps/catalog direct imports' and 'keep as documented seam'.
proposed_action: Report-only. If flipped to remove: rewrite all `@/lib/apps` importers to `@/lib/apps/catalog`, then delete the barrel. Otherwise keep as intentional public API.

title: Template 'Types/Component/Export' banner comments in short RDNA components
summary: The RDNA component template stamps `// ======== Types ========`, `// ======== Component ========`, and sometimes `// ======== Export ========` banners into every component regardless of size. In files <150 lines with a single interface + single component, the banners are pure scaffolding. Seven `// Export` banners sit directly above a trivial object+default-export pair. Keep banners in files >200 lines with 3+ sections.
proposed_action: Strip banners from files <150 lines with a single interface + single component. Drop the `// Export` banner wherever it sits above fewer than 5 lines of exports. Leave banners intact in large composite files (AppWindow, ContextMenu, Menubar, etc.).

title: Thin *App.tsx wrappers exist solely to expose default exports to lazy()
summary: StudioApp is `export { default } from './studio/PixelArtEditor'` — no logic. GoodNewsApp is an alias re-export of GoodNewsLegacyApp. ManifestoApp renders `<ManifestoBook />` with no props forwarded. Each exists only because catalog.tsx uses `lazy(() => import('@/components/apps/XxxApp'))`. Collapsing would let lazy() target the deeper module directly. The indirection may be intentional (decouples catalog from internals); reporting at low confidence.
proposed_action: Report-only. If approved: update catalog.tsx lazy() imports to point at the underlying default-export modules (./manifesto/ManifestoBook, ./goodnews/GoodNewsLegacyApp, ./studio/PixelArtEditor) and delete the three wrapper files.

title: Low-value '// Main' / '// Helpers' dividers in short generator scripts
summary: Two generator scripts decorate `Helpers` and `Main` sections with `// ----` banners. Both files are imperative pipelines (parse-input → transform → write-output); banners add minor visual noise without aiding navigation.
proposed_action: Remove the four banner comments (both are two-line banners). Scripts still read clearly as top-to-bottom procedures.
